title: gymnolaena oaxacana
intro: Gymnolaena oaxacana, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and easing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
